A Swedish Perspective on the Prevention of Moisture Problems During the Building’s Design Phase Stephen Burke 1 and Veronica Yverås 2 1 Department of Building Physics, Lund Institute of Technology, Box 118, 221 00, Lund, Sweden stephen.burke@byggtek.lth.se 2 Department of Building Physics, Chalmers University of Technology, Sweden veronica.yveras@jm.se Abstract.Moisture problems in buildings are increasingly being reported in the mass media in Sweden, often leading to some controversial stories

This paper explores this question by interviewing some engineering consultants on how they evaluate the performance of a building, and to what extent knowledge about building physics theory is being used during the design process to prevent moisture problems from occurring.

Moisture design is a method used by building physicists to determine the risk of a building developing moisture problems in the future if it is utilized during the design phase of the construction process.
The primary method under consideration is a detailed case study of one company to determine what effects the tool has had on the company's decision- making process when planning a building.

This project will entail the identification of building physics areas having the largest potential impact on the decision-making process, the design of a tools package for use in the sector, the testing of this package by companies and the analysis of results.
can reduce the risk of both short and long-term problems and energy costs by addressing aspects of building physics early in the construction process.
